title: Virecta pratensis

The genus Virecta is a group of flowering plants native to tropical Africa and Asia. This species, Virecta pratensis, is a member of the order Gentianales, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The classification of Virecta pratensis is further supported by its unique characteristics and morphological features.
uses: Virecta pratensis, also known as the blue spotted virecta, has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. Its roots are said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions.
